texts.full_text: of this order is the bristlemouth Cyclothone pygmaeae. Native to the Mediterranean Sea, it reaches just 1.5cm (0.6 in) as an adult. The largest species is the barbeled dragonfish Opostomias micripnis, widely found in theAtlantic, Indian and Pacific Oceans and measuring about in adult length. These fish have a highly unusual and often almost nightmarish appearance. They all have teeth on the premaxilla and maxilla. Their maxillary ligaments, as well as some muscles and certain bones in the branchial cavity, are specialized in a distinctive way. Most have large mouths extending back past the eyes. Some also have a chin barbel. The dorsal and/or pectoral fins are missing in some, but others have an adiposefin. The pelvic fin has 4-9 rays, and the stomiiformes possess 5-24 branchiostegal rays. Their scales are cycloid, delicate and easily sloughed off; some are scaleless. The coloration is typically dark brown or black; a few (mostly Gonostomatoidei) are silver, and photophores (light-producing organs) are common in this order. The teeth of stomiiformes are often transparent and non-reflective

texts.full_text: we’re the perfect mommy-hero (or big brother, best friend, etc.) that this littlest part of us has been waiting for all along! Xox What I’m talking about is also very different also from the kind of sadness that is like a tantrum (not wanting things to be what they are) or regret (wishing you had behaved differently toward them).It is critically important to acknowledge that hurting part of ourselves, too, and inquire about our mind’s assertion that reality should be different–or would have been better some others way. When you know how to question these thoughts (let me know if you want help) thins can shift instantly. It truly all can be experienced as love when you start to notice; when all along we were brought up to think it was sadness. This was my little Memorial Day gift (that I wanted to send to new folks on my list); it’s for anyone others who has lost a loved one. To help us know that once we clear up the thoughts that separate us, our loved ones are always with us–in ways that are different from and yet can be as good as or even better than the past (i.e. each day whether they are here in the flesh or not!). Just sharing with other sons and daughters and mothers and siblings and friends and lovers what has helped me stay super connected and even very happy in the face of change and loss. xoxo Spread the love Post navigation A Thread that’s Soft and Golden… Ushering my Poppy Out with Love Same Old #&%$ in Your Relationships? One of the reasons for the difference in patterns is the difference in the way hair follicles are distributed on the scalp. Men have more hair follicles on the sides of the scalp, which is why they tend to experience a receding hairline. Women, on the other hand, have a more even distribution of hair follicles on the scalp, which is why they experience a more even loss of hair. Another factor that contributes to the difference in baldness patterns is the difference in the way hair follicles respond to hormones. Men experience a decrease in testosterone levels as they age, which can lead to the loss of hair Women, on the other hand, experience a decrease in estrogen levels as they age, which can also lead to the loss of hair follicles. It is to note that there are other factors that can contribute to hair loss, such as genetics, stress, and medical conditions. To investigate the expression of caspase-3 and iNOS in different intervals and to provide evidence for estimation of injury intervals after brain contusion in human. Thirty cases died of serious brain injury were included into the injury groups and 5 cases died of non-brain injury were served as control group. to analyze the changes of caspase-3 and iNOS expression in brain samples at different intervals (2h, 4-8h, 10-14h, 1-2d, 3-5d, 8-11d) by immunohistochemistry and auto-image analysis system.The study aimed to investigate the time-dependent expression patterns of caspase-3 and iNOS in contused human brain tissue samples from individuals who died due to severe traumatic brain injury (TBI). Thirty patients with fatal TBI were included as part of the experimental groups, while five subjects without any history or evidence of neurological trauma served as a control group. The primary objective was to elucidate how these two key proteins—caspase-3 and iNOS—are expressed at various time points post-injury. Immunohistochemical analysis combined with an auto-image analysis system were employed for the assessment of caspase-3 and iNOS expression in brain samples collected from different intervals following injury: 2 hours, 4 to 8 hours, 10 to 14 hours, 1 to 2 days, 3 to 5 days, and finally between 8 to 11 days post-injury. This comprehensive approach allowed for the detailed examination of protein expression dynamics over a critical period following brain contusion. The findings from this study are expected to contribute valuable insights into understanding temporal changes in molecular markers associated with TBI pathOlogy, potentially aiding clinicians i

We were admitted to labor and delivery that night and they started me on cytotec to induce labor. It took over 24 hours until we finally delivered our 5oz baby boy. texts.full_text: . We observed that the maximum CPY production occurred in the early stationary phase, suggesting that the cells had to reach a certain metabolic state to achieve the highest CPY yield. This growth-phase-dependent production is likely due to the increased demand for energy and nutrients in the stationary phase, leading to enhanced promoter activity and CPY production. To further enhance CPY production, we optimized the growth conditions. We found that the presence of a carbon source, such as glucose, during the induction phase was crucial for maximal CPY production. This is likely because glucose represses the GAL1 promotEr, preventing premature induction of CPY production during the exponential phase. Additionally, the presence of a nitrogen source, such as ammonium sulfate, during the induction phase was also essential, as nitrogen is a key component of the CPY protein. We also investigated the effect of temperature on CPY production. We found that a shift to a lower temperature, such as 20°C, during the induction phase resulted in a significant increase in CPY production. This is likely due to the lower temperature causing a reduction in the rate of protein degradation, leading to the accumulation of CPY. In conclusion, the production of CPY in S. cerevisiae can be significantly enhanced by optimizing the growth conditions, including the presence of a carbon source, a nitrogen source, and a lower induction temperature. These optimizations, in combination with the use of a strongly regulated promoter and a vpl1 mutant strain, can lead to the regulated overproduction and secretion of yeast carboxypeptidase

texts.full_text: classicism. Concentrating on his drawing and emphasizing the outlines of figures, he painted works suchasBlonde Bather (1881 and 1882) and The Large Bathers (1884–87; Philadelphia Museum of Art) during what issometimes called his "Ingres period". After 1890 he changed direction again. To dissolve outlines, as in his earlier work, he returned to thinly brushed color. From this period onward he concentrated on monumental nudesand domestic scenes, fine examples of which are Girls at the Piano, 1892, and Grandes Baigneuses, 1887. The latter painting is the most typical and successful of Renoir's late, abundantly fleshed nudes. A prolific artist, he created several thousand paintings. The warm sensuality of Renoir's style made his paintings some of the most well-known and frequently reproduced works in the history of art. The single largest collection of his works—181 paintings in all—is at the Barnes Foundation, inPhiladelphia. Catalogue raisonné A five-volume catalogue raisonné of Renoir's works (with one supplement) was published by Bernheim-Jeune between 1983 and 2014. Bernheim-Jeune is the only surviving major art dealer that was used by Renoir. The Wildenstein Institute is preparing, but has not yet published, a critical catalogue of Renoir's work. Methods for statistical and experimental adjustment of these spuriously influenced correlations are reviewed and compared, but are rejected in favor of Bashaw and Anderson's (1967) reliability solution. To illustrate the proposed correction and to show the potential magnitude Corrections for spurious influences on correlations between MMPI scales are essential to ensure accurate and reliable results in psychological assessments. When measures contain common elements, the correlations between them are prone to spurious influences due to the overlapping items. For instance, both the Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory (MMPI) and the California Psychological Inventory (CPI) share common items in their respective subscales. Consequently, the correlations between these subscales are inadvertently influenced by their overlapping items. To address these spurious influences, various statistical and experimental adjustment methods have been proposed and reviewed. However, these methods are ultimately rejected in favor of the reliability solution proposed by Bashaw and Anderson in 1967. This solution

She was one of the first employees at the US website Codecademy, which raised $10 million in funding to offer free programming lessons online; later she became the poster girl for the profession when she co-founded the global network Rails Girls in 2010, a not-for-profit organisation that teaches women to create web applications. Liukas’s Hello Ruby illustrations are designed to stimulate children’s interest in computer programming (LINDA LUIKA) Rails Girls started as a weekend course for her friends – she aimed to demystify coding by teaching the participants to use a web toolbox called Ruby on Rails, an open-source web application created in 2004. It is rather like painting by numbers but with code. ‘It lets you build something visible and real very fast,’ Liukas says. ‘It’s beautiful.’ Crucially, Rails Girls classes take place in the real world, not only online. The organisation, which is run by volunteers, now has more than 10,000 women aged 11 to 65 involved around the world, in countries from Germany to Mozambique. ‘Normal people who don’t interact with the programming community think code is written by computers for computers, but it’s not,’ she says. ‘Code is written by human beings for other human beings; it’s profoundly human.’ Liukas grew up in Helsinki, where she lives now with her ‘long-suffering’ boyfriend. texts.full_text: stamens are short, with orange anthers. The long thin fruits are also reminiscent of Eschscholzia. The genus is named for English botanist and collector John Hunnemann (1760-1839). Biomasses of cyanobacteria are used as biofertilizers for the improvement of nutrient or mineral status and water-holding capacity of the soil. The secondary metabolites of cyanobacteria are used in pharmaceuticals, nutraceutical and chemical industries. In the industrial sector, value-added products from cyanobacteria such as pigments, enzymes and exopolysaccharides are being produced in large scales for biomedical and health applications. Age-old applications of cyanobacteria in agriculture and industry have evolved with advancements in biotechnology, leading to the development of innovative and sustainable practices. For instance, in agriculture, cyanobacteria can enhance soil fertility through nitrogen fixation, which reduces the need for chemical fertilizers. This not only lowers costs for farmers but also minimizes environmental impacts associated with excessive fertilizer use. In the industrial sector, the production of biofuels from cyanobacteria is gaining attention as a sustainable alternative to fossil fuels. Cyanobacteria can convert sunlight and carbon dioxide into lipids, which can be processed into biodiesel. Additionally, the ability of cyanobacteria to produce hydrogen through photosynthesis offers another promising avenue for renewable energy. Moreover, the bioremediation capabilities of cyanobacteria are being harnessed in environmental management practices. they can detoxify contaminated soils and waters by removing or transforming pollutants,
